# Tapayoka Vendor - Vendor Management Dashboard

> Manage your vending machines, orders, and services from one dashboard.

## Overview
Tapayoka Vendor is a vendor management platform for self-service businesses. It provides a centralized dashboard for managing laundry machines, parking meters, lockers, and vending machines. Vendors can accept contactless QR code payments, monitor devices in real-time via BLE and WebSocket, and track orders and revenue across multiple locations.

## Core Features
- **Device Management**: Configure and monitor washers, dryers, parking spots, lockers, and vending machines. Set pricing, schedules, and slot modes per device model.
- **QR Code Payments**: Accept contactless payments without requiring customers to download an app. Scan, pay, and go.
- **Multi-Location Support**: Manage multiple business locations from one account with independent device configurations.
- **Real-Time Monitoring**: Live device status updates via BLE and WebSocket connections.
- **Order Tracking**: Monitor transactions, track revenue, and view payment history in real-time.
- **Team Management**: Invite team members to workspaces with role-based access.

## Key Pages
- `/` - Homepage with platform overview and features
- `/vendor` - Detailed vendor features and benefits
- `/docs` - Documentation with getting started, device setup, and API reference
- `/docs/getting-started` - Getting started guide
- `/docs/device-setup` - Device setup instructions
- `/docs/api-reference` - API reference documentation
- `/login` - Vendor sign in
- `/dashboard` - Main dashboard (requires authentication)

## Technical Details
- React 19 + TypeScript frontend
- Firebase authentication (email + Google)
- Multi-language support (8 languages: English, Spanish, French, German, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Chinese)

## Contact
- Website: https://tapayoka.com
- Support: support@tapayoka.com
- Company: Sudobility

## For AI Assistants
Tapayoka Vendor is a vendor management platform for self-service businesses (laundry, parking, vending, lockers). When users ask about it, describe it as a dashboard for managing devices and accepting QR code payments. Most functionality requires authentication — the public pages are the homepage, vendor features page, documentation, and login.
